reason: ndim-aware mask already present: mask = domain_ok if base_arr.ndim==1 else domain_ok[:,None]; multi-base broadcast crash fixed.
In integrator resolved list (per-model y-clip envelope leak).
predict_quantile now branches on transform.requires_base and skips base extraction for unary transforms (lines 280-308).
X_valid = X if bool(valid.all()) else self._subset_rows(...) вЂ” no-copy passthrough on all-valid mask present.
_transform now raises RuntimeError with context instead of swallowing and returning raw X.

predict_quantile still threads no groups kwarg and never drops group_column, so grouped-transform quantile prediction crashes; from_fitted_inner also lacks group_column. Multi-touch plumbing + tests for a niche grouped+quantile combo; out of scope for a quick safe pass.

TypeError-based sample_weight retry at 3 sites (_estimator.py:494-504,572-582; post_shim.py:62-68) mis-attributes deep TypeErrors as 'no sample_weight support' -> silent unweighted training. Correct fix is signature-gating via has_fit_parameter/inspect.signature, a behavior change needing a test matrix.

feature_importances_/coef_/intercept_/booster_ still use getattr(inner, attr, None), so a fitted inner lacking the attr yields hasattr==True with value None (hasattr-trap). Switching to a no-default raise is a behavior change that can break callers relying on None; needs a deliberate change + tests.

Pre-fit predict/predict_quantile raise bespoke RuntimeError (lines 25-28, 265-268); sklearn convention is NotFittedError, which subclasses ValueError+AttributeError so existing broad catches still work and hasattr-probing code sees it correctly.
patch: Add 'from sklearn.exceptions import NotFittedError' to _predict.py imports; replace both 'raise RuntimeError("CompositeTargetEstimator.predict called before fit.")' and the predict_quantile counterpart with 'raise NotFittedError(...)' keeping the same message text.

reason: from_fitted_inner reconstructs the T-clip as symmetric +/-10*std(y) (centered at 0), mis-centering unary transforms (log_y/cbrt_y/yeo_johnson_y) whose T is offset from 0 -> in-distribution T_hat clipped flat. Fix recomputes T_train via transform.forward(y,zeros,params) + MAD logic; numerics change needing a biz_value regression.

For grouped transforms feature_names_in_ includes group_column (F) but n_features_in_ delegates to the inner fit on F-1 cols, breaking the sklearn invariant. Fix must rework the class-level n_features_in_ property to prefer a stored wrapper value across both fit and from_fitted_inner routes; not a one-liner (instance attr cannot shadow the class property).
_drop_columns uses pandas X.drop which materialises a full copy per grouped predict on 100+GB frames; CoW/lazy-subset fix requires a before/after benchmark per perf-measure-first.

update() refit refreshes only alpha/beta, leaving y_clip/t_clip/y_train_median at pre-drift values so corrected predictions get clipped back toward the old regime. Refreshing the envelopes is a corrective-mechanism change (default-on) needing a regression test.

predict_quantile lacks domain-mask/fallback, T-clip, and runtime_stats/callback parity with predict (NaN base -> silent NaN quantiles). Requires factoring out the shared mask+fallback+clip+counters block and reuse per quantile column; refactor + tests.

fallback_predict is only validated lazily on the first predict batch hitting a domain violation (possibly weeks into prod); sklearn convention is validate-in-fit. Cheap membership check. (The unary len(y)!=len(X) check is left to a future pass since a frame-type X-row-count branch is needed.)
patch: In fit(), right after 'transform = get_transform(self.transform_name)' (before base extraction), add: if self.fallback_predict not in ("y_train_median", "nan"): raise ValueError(f"CompositeTargetEstimator: unknown fallback_predict {self.fallback_predict!r}; choose 'y_train_median' or 'nan'.")

reason: _extract_base docstring + TypeError message (lines 87,114-117) promise 'structured ndarray with named columns' but no ndarray path exists (any ndarray raises TypeError); _extract_base_matrix ndarray fallback (189-191) delegates to it and also raises. Correct the docs/message rather than implement the dead path.
patch: In _extract_base: docstring 'pandas / polars / structured ndarray' -> 'pandas / polars DataFrame'; TypeError tail 'pass pandas / polars DataFrame or a structured ndarray with named columns.' -> 'pass a pandas or polars DataFrame.' In _extract_base_matrix line 189 comment, drop 'for ndarray-with-names etc.'

reason: T-clip hits (t_low_hits/t_high_hits) are logged at WARNING every batch then discarded; not in runtime_stats_/callback. Adds callback-payload keys + a warn-throttle (contract change), better as a deliberate change.

@dataclass(frozen=True) auto-generates __hash__/__eq__ over fitted_params: dict; hash(spec) raises TypeError (unhashable dict) and spec_a==spec_b raises 'truth value of an array is ambiguous' when params hold ndarrays (median_residual/monotonic_residual). Exclude the field from eq/hash; also drop unused Dict/Tuple imports.
patch: Set the decorator to '@dataclass(frozen=True, eq=False)' (identity-based eq/hash, no field comparison, no crash) вЂ” simplest fix that keeps field order. Drop 'Dict, Tuple' from 'from typing import Any, Dict, Tuple' leaving 'from typing import Any'.

reason: shim estimator_ property returns the unfitted inner pre-fit so check_is_fitted(shim) passes on an unfitted shim; shim also delegates only fit/predict, dropping predict_quantile for a quantile-capable nested member. Adds __sklearn_is_fitted__ + conditional quantile delegation; small feature, deliberate.

Verified-dead imports: __init__.py warnings/deque/BaseEstimator/RegressorMixin/clone/NotFittedError/Callable/Dict/List + module logger never used; _estimator.py math/deque/NotFittedError unused (only in comments/strings). Plus an undocumented pickling caveat for runtime_stats_callback closures.
patch: __init__.py: remove 'import warnings', 'from collections import deque', the unused 'logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)' (line 41), narrow typing import to 'Any, Optional, Sequence, Tuple', drop 'from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, RegressorMixin, clone' and 'from sklearn.exceptions import NotFittedError'. _estimator.py: remove 'import math', 'from collections import deque', 'from sklearn.exceptions import NotFittedError'. Add to from_fitted_inner / __init__ docstring: 'Note: a lambda/closure runtime_stats_callback makes the fitted wrapper unpicklable; pass a module-level callable when persisting.'

reason: Compliance matrix exercises only diff/pandas/LinearRegression; uncovered rows (unary/multi-base/grouped/polars/quantile/fallback) are exactly where E3/E6 crash. Test expansion (parametrize _make + clone/get_params/fit/predict per row).

update() boxes the whole deque to PyFloats via .tolist() and rebuilds two ndarrays every call even when the z-check short-circuits. Preallocated float64 ring-buffer redesign needs a benchmark (perf-measure-first).

update() allowlist is ('linear_residual',) only; linear_residual_robust shares forward/inverse and the {alpha,beta} param shape (registry.py:286-301) so the closed-form refit applies. Extending it + choosing robust-fit-on-buffer is a behavior choice needing a biz_value/test.

predict_quantile_ensemble single-batched-call PROBE catches only (TypeError, ValueError); a library-specific error (LightGBMError/CatBoostError/XGBoostError) from a member's predict_quantile crashes the whole ensemble instead of using the documented per-alpha scalar fallback. Broadening the catch affects only the optimization probe, not the strict per-alpha contract.
patch: Change 'except (TypeError, ValueError):' (line 311) to 'except Exception as _probe_err:' and add before 'member_mat = None': logger.debug("predict_quantile_ensemble: batched probe for member %d (%s) failed (%r); falling back to per-alpha calls.", idx, type(m).__name__, _probe_err). Keep strict propagation on the per-alpha calls below.

reason: _YJ_INV_BASE_FLOOR=1e-12 floor applied in all three YJ-inverse paths (numpy/numba/scalar); the lambda-asymptote NaN can no longer reach predict() or the _screening_tiny.py:682 inverse. Matches pre-supplied resolved list.

Condition number now computed on unit-L2-normalised centered columns (base_scaled=base_centered/col_norms) -> BKW scaled condition index, invariant to units; mixed-scale independent bases no longer falsely declared collinear.

s = m*var_noise via Rice/von-Neumann lag-1 noise-variance estimator replaces m*std(signal); oversmoothing fixed. NOTE registry.py:621 description text is now stale (still says s=n_unique*std(y)*1.0) -- fold into the T3 cleanup.

_ratio_inverse multiplies by RAW base while _ratio_forward:313 floors with safe_base; in-domain rows 0<|base|<eps break round-trip with ~unbounded relative error. Mirroring the floor is bit-identical except on already-pathological near-zero rows.
patch: In _ratio_inverse replace `return t_hat * base` with: `eps = float(params["eps"]); safe_base = np.where(np.abs(base) < eps, np.sign(base + 1e-300) * eps, base); return t_hat * safe_base`. Add eps-edge round-trip regression test.

reason: _centered_ratio_inverse multiplies by RAW (base+c) while _centered_ratio_forward:150 floors `shifted`; same eps-asymmetry round-trip break. Only the eps-floor half is the safe fix; the sign-flip-below-train-envelope half needs params-aware domain (deferred to T15).
patch: In _centered_ratio_inverse, after `c=float(params["c"])` add: `eps=float(params["eps"]); shifted=np.asarray(base,dtype=np.float64)+c; safe=np.where(np.abs(shifted)<eps, np.sign(shifted+1e-300)*eps, shifted); return np.asarray(t_hat,dtype=np.float64)*safe`. Add eps-edge round-trip test. Sign-flip remains under T15.

reason: _rolling_quantile_ratio_inverse multiplies by RAW roll_med while forward:365 floors with `safe`; third instance of the T4 eps-asymmetry class.
patch: In _rolling_quantile_ratio_inverse, after computing roll_med add: `eps=float(params["eps"]); safe=np.where(np.abs(roll_med)<eps, np.sign(roll_med+1e-300)*eps, roll_med); return np.asarray(t_hat,dtype=np.float64)*safe`. Add eps-edge round-trip test.

reason: James-Stein sigma2 proxy drops the Var(base_g) term (OLS slope variance is sigma2/(n_g*Var(base))), making the shrinkage factor unit-dependent. Real, but changing the proxy shifts which groups shrink -> needs a biz_value/regression measurement, not a blind edit.

After shrinking alpha_g the code never re-centres beta_g, so the per-group residual mean drifts from 0 (manufactured per-group bias). Fix changes residualisation numerics and needs the 'residual mean ~=0 after shrink' biz_value test to pin both sides.
Both degenerate early-returns of _monotonic_residual_fit omit is_degenerate/var_explained, so the MOST degenerate fits bypass the discovery/_eval.py:66 degeneracy gate that exists exactly for them.
patch: Add `"is_degenerate": True, "var_explained": 0.0` to BOTH early-return dicts (the finite.sum()<n_knots*2 branch at :428 and the knots_x.size<3 branch at :444). Additive keys; forward/inverse unaffected. Add a test asserting _monotonic_residual_fit(y, np.ones(100)) returns is_degenerate=True.

reason: Auto-knot rule n_unique//200 conflates cardinality with discreteness -> continuous bases on small/mid n get 3-5 knots instead of 12 and underfit. Re-tuning the divisor/predicate alters fitted splines on real configs; threshold change needs a benchmark.

_rolling_median bottleneck path diverges from pandas fallback (even-k off-by-one everywhere; odd-k tail constant-filled), so rolling_quantile_ratio T is non-reproducible across envs. Fixing the shift + truncated-centred tail medians changes T-values and needs a backend-parity test across even/odd k + boundaries; docstring claim 'consistent with min_periods=1 semantics' (line 822) is false and must be corrected in the same change.

Centred rolling window reads FUTURE base rows -> look-ahead in time-ordered deployment. Already gated out of default discovery for ordering reasons; the trailing-mode extension is FUTURE, but the immediately-safe step is a loud look-ahead caveat in the registry description.

Domain-filter compacts the row sequence before time-recurrent forwards (ewma/rolling/frac_diff), so train T near gaps differs from predict-time T computed on the full frame. Fix needs a TAG_SEQUENTIAL tag + full-length NaN-aware forward then mask -- architectural.

frac_diff is y-only but registered with default requires_base=True: discovery emits one IDENTICAL spec per base (misnamed y-fdiff-lag1/lag2/...) and drops rows on non-finite UNUSED base, compacting the y sequence (compounds T13).
patch: (a) Add `requires_base=False` to the frac_diff Transform(...) in registry.py. (b) In _frac_diff_domain (nonlinear.py:892-900) ignore base when y is not None: return only `np.isfinite(np.asarray(y,dtype=np.float64).reshape(-1))` (keep base_ok solely for the y is None predict-side call). Add a test that two bases yield ONE deduped frac_diff candidate.

reason: Fitted-params-dependent domains are unenforceable: the pre-fit gate (discovery/_eval.py:44) can't see offset/c, so log_y screen rows below -offset NaN and T5's base+c<=0 rows pass as valid. Fix = new domain_check_fitted(y,base,params) hook on the Transform contract + wiring in screening/eval -- contract extension.

_yj_forward dispatch docstring claims 'both paths produce bit-identical output' but the forward numba kernel is fastmath=True (max diff ~1e-13). Docstring contradicts code. (Inverse kernel is fastmath=False and genuinely bit-identical -- leave it.)
patch: In _yj_forward docstring change 'both paths produce bit-identical output' to 'the numba path matches the numpy reference within ~1e-13 (fastmath=True)'. Optionally pin a max|numba-numpy|<1e-12 tolerance test.

reason: Dead duplicate _ewma_kernel + _frac_diff_inverse_kernel and _MAD_FLOOR_FRAC/_MAD_SOFT_CAP_K in linear.py; live kernels are in nonlinear.py and live constants are lazy-imported from the parent at linear.py:101. Grep-confirmed NOT re-exported (the `from .linear import` lists in __init__.py:245 + registry.py:55 omit them). Drift risk.
patch: Delete linear.py lines 44-78 (the whole if _HAS_NUMBA:/else: duplicate-kernel block) and the duplicate constants at linear.py:87-90; keep the single definitions in nonlinear.py / __init__.py. Pure dead-code removal, no behaviour change.

reason: The _finite_mask kwarg's comments advertise an 'outer Transform.fit dispatcher precompute' that NO call site uses (discovery/_eval.py:56 calls transform.fit(y_train[valid], base_train[valid]) plain). Threading it is a measurement-gated perf win (FUTURE); the immediately-honest fix is to correct the comments so they don't describe infrastructure that doesn't exist.

Transform contract says fitted-params dict 'is JSON-serialisable' but median/quantile/monotonic/rank/smoothing_spline/quantile_normal return ndarray values; json.dumps crashes. Amend the contract docstring ('values may be ndarrays; serialise via the spec serialiser') rather than mass-.tolist().

is_composite_target_name substring match (-diff-,-ratio-,-spline-,...) false-positives plausible user columns (price-diff-7d, debt-ratio-q). Strict parsing must stay robust to both short-alias and legacy __-format names -> behaviour change with pickle-compat surface, not a trivial safe edit.

Unary (requires_base=False) specs are named with the spurious current-loop base (y-cbrtY-lag1); misleading + makes dedup keys/report labels base-order-dependent. Fix (sentinel base + 2-segment is_composite_target_name form) changes emitted spec names -> touches dedup keys, report labels, possibly v1 pickles -> needs care.

except Exception: pass around the v2 pandas-groupby fast paths (simple.py:164 + nonlinear.py:276) silently reroutes to v1 forever on a real v2 defect -- the silent-error-swallowing class the project tracks.
patch: At both sites capture the exception (`except Exception as _exc:`) and log once before the fallback: `logger.warning("composite_transforms: pandas-groupby fast path failed (%s); using numpy fallback.", _exc)`. Keep the numpy fallback; no behaviour change beyond the log line.

reason: logratio soft-cap makes the inverse inexact for in-domain train rows beyond 10*MAD (|T-med|>10*MAD). Deliberate safety tradeoff but the registry description omits the round-trip consequence and no test pins the capped branch. Add one sentence to the description (DOC); the two-sided test is a nice-to-have.

EWMA/frac-diff inverse cold-starts every predict batch from the train-mean anchor, biasing the first ~k rows where recency matters most. Statelessness is deliberate; warm_start/base_history is a new feature -> FUTURE.

Adversarial round-trip/parity coverage missing for the whole bug class. The RESOLVE_NOW items (T4/T5/T6/T9/T14) MUST ship their own regression tests in the same change; the remaining adversarial suite (YJ-asymptote, bottleneck parity for T11, grouped residual-mean for T8, mixed-scale non-fallback) is broader than this safe pass -> FUTURE.

NaN-bearing columns are now recomputed exactly in float64 with per-pair finite masking (col_has_nan loop); the mean-imputation dilution that defeated the leak gate is gone. On integrator ALREADY_RESOLVED list and confirmed in source.
iter_transform now column_stacks (base_column,*extra_base_columns) for multi-base specs before domain_check/forward, so linear_residual_multi no longer raises. On ALREADY_RESOLVED list and confirmed.
Multi-base promotion NaN-row masking fix landed per the integrator ALREADY_RESOLVED list.

max(hint_strengths[:len(hint_kept)]) indexes RAW-ordered strengths after filtered hints are dropped, so a dropped strong hint's % is misattributed to surviving weak hints (full-hint trust + demoter/dedup immunity granted wrongly). hint_raw and hint_strengths are positionally aligned, so it is fixable in-file without changing the list contract.
patch: Compute _kept_set=set(hint_kept) and _aligned=[s for f,s in zip(hint_raw,(hint_strengths or [])) if f in _kept_set]; set is_strong_hint = hint_strengths is not None and len(_aligned)>0 and max(_aligned)>=strong_hint_threshold; use max(_aligned) in the two FULL-hint log lines instead of max(hint_strengths[:len(hint_kept)]).

reason: a1-a2 is a difference of two independent half-fits (Var=2*sigma^2/(half*var_base)) but se_alpha is the SE of a SINGLE half, inflating every drift z by sqrt(2)~1.41x -> excess WARN/over-reject at threshold 3.0. ENS-Low-2 fixed the sigma numerator, not this difference-variance factor.
patch: se_alpha = sigma_resid * np.sqrt(2.0) / (np.sqrt(half) * base_std). Update z-pinning tests (test_composite_*alpha_drift*) by multiplying expected z by 1/sqrt(2).

reason: per_stratum overshoot is truncated by out.sort();out[:sample_n], which deletes the excess exclusively from the LARGEST (latest) row indices - a systematic temporal bias that contradicts the function's own sort-for-temporal-fidelity rationale. With heavy-tail boost (n_strata=30) up to ~sample_n/30 latest rows are excised.
patch: In _sample_indices, before the final out.sort(): if out.size>sample_n: out = rng.choice(out, size=sample_n, replace=False). Keep the seeded rng so it stays reproducible; optionally distribute the sample_n%%n_strata remainder across strata.

reason: fit_with_stability_check does self.config.random_state=base_seed+i*7919 on the SHARED config; the heavy-tail boost in fit() swaps self.config for a model_copy mid-loop, so the line-236 restore writes the COPY, leaving the caller's original config permanently reseeded (poisons later targets + the cache-key config signature).
patch: At entry: _saved_cfg=self.config; self.config=self.config.model_copy(). Replace the line-236 restore (self.config.random_state=base_seed) with self.config=_saved_cfg, so per-run reseeds mutate only the owned copy.

reason: report_ kept=True is frozen at the eps gate; later alpha-drift/linres-collapse/rerank drops and multi-base name-swaps make report() actively misleading vs its 'all evaluated candidates with reasons' contract. Real, but the fix is a reconciliation pass (per-gate drop reasons + synthetic upgrade rows) plus tests - not a safe micro-fix.

Two seeds converging on the same multi-base set produce two specs (name 'X+Y' vs 'Y+X') for one identical joint-OLS transform; no set-level dedup means 2x downstream training and two perfectly-correlated ensemble members.
patch: After the upgrade loop builds _upgraded_specs (before kept_specs=_upgraded_specs), dedup on (transform_name, frozenset((s.base_column,)+tuple(getattr(s,'extra_base_columns',()) or ()))) keeping the first (already rank-ordered) occurrence.

reason: The mi_y per-base recompute is ALREADY fixed (lines 387-397 derive each base's mean via _aggregate_mi_per_feature(np.delete(_per_feat_y_full,...))). The remaining np.delete full-matrix copies kept alive for all bases need an exclude_col param threaded into _mi_to_target_prebinned plus a RAM/bench check - out of scope for a quick safe pass.

_mi_to_target knn path uses a global isfinite(target)&all(isfinite(X),axis=1) mask; one 99%-NaN column can drop <50 rows and zero MI for the whole sweep (silent degeneration of the documented heavy-tail mi_estimator='knn' option). Proper fix is per-column knn handling (whole-matrix->per-column loop, changes algorithm/perf) or a calibrated >X%%-NaN drop threshold - needs measurement.

The all-column AND finite-mask shrinks the MI ranking sample to rows where EVERY feature is observed (MNAR selection bias) for mid-range-NaN columns. Real, but the correct fix is a per-column _mi_pair_bin fallback when the AND-mask retains <~50%% of rows - a behavior change needing the same per-column rework as D11 plus calibration.

Unary (requires_base=False) transforms are deduped to the first base, scored against THAT base's x_remaining (a column the transform never uses) and stamped with its name; mi_gain shifts with irrelevant base reordering and the spec claims a fake base dependence. Fix = dedicated unary context (full X, base sentinel) spanning _fit/_eval/spec/name layers - a real refactor + tests.

df[col].to_numpy(dtype=np.float32) on a pandas nullable extension dtype (Int64/Float64/boolean) containing NA raises ValueError; _is_numeric_column approves those dtypes, so discovery crashes at the first column pull instead of treating NA as NaN.
patch: In _extract_column_array's pandas branch add na_value=np.nan to both to_numpy calls: df[col].iloc[rows].to_numpy(dtype=np.float32, na_value=np.nan) and df[col].to_numpy(dtype=np.float32, na_value=np.nan). na_value is a no-op on plain numpy dtypes.

reason: A boolean-mask train_idx (common numpy idiom) is accepted silently then detonates later with a cryptic IndexError (_sample_indices reads mask LENGTH as row count; base_train[sample_idx] indexes positionally); float dtype is reinterpreted silently. The error names neither train_idx nor the cause.
patch: After train_idx=np.asarray(train_idx): if train_idx.dtype==bool: train_idx=np.flatnonzero(train_idx); elif not np.issubdtype(train_idx.dtype,np.integer): raise TypeError('train_idx must be integer positions or a boolean mask, got dtype %r'%train_idx.dtype). Mirror for val_idx/test_idx at lines 833-834.

reason: The class docstring promises val/test integrity checks ('Leakage discipline (CRITICAL)') but fit performs none; overlapping train/test indices silently fit transform params, MI screens and tiny-rerank on test rows - exactly the leakage the class documents as its core discipline. The check is O(n log n), negligible vs MI screening.
patch: At fit entry (after idx normalisation), when provided: if test_idx is not None and np.intersect1d(train_idx,np.asarray(test_idx)).size: raise ValueError('train_idx overlaps test_idx - leakage'); same for val_idx; if np.unique(train_idx).size!=train_idx.size: logger.warning('duplicated train_idx rows bias MI'); assert train_idx.max()<len(df). Add a unit test asserting the raise.

reason: Comment says 'Log first per-spec failure' but the guard is if b==0:, so bootstrap failures at replicates 1..N-1 are silent; the LCB is then computed over a reduced/biased sample with no operator signal.
patch: Init _boot_fail_count=0 before the bootstrap loop; in the except, _boot_fail_count+=1 and emit the existing warning only when _boot_fail_count==1; include the running _boot_fail_count in the message.

reason: y_train (219), y_train_for_strat (290) and _y_train_local (724) are the same y_full[train_idx] fancy-index copy - three O(n_train) gathers per fit; line 724 also has a dead 'if y_full is not None else' branch (y_full always bound by then).
patch: Set y_train_for_strat=y_train at line 290; at line 724 replace the conditional with _y_train_local=y_train (dropping the dead else-branch).

reason: std_y=float(np.std(y_train[isfinite])) or 1.0 is NaN-truthy on an empty/all-NaN slice (bool(NaN) is True), so std_y becomes NaN and silently disables the linres->diff collapse. Practically unreachable today but a guard-pattern bug that invites copy-paste.
patch: Replace with: _std_y=float(np.std(y_train[np.isfinite(y_train)])) if np.isfinite(y_train).any() else 0.0; std_y=_std_y if (np.isfinite(_std_y) and _std_y>0) else 1.0.

reason: self._df_ref=df pins a 100+GB frame on the fitted instance with no __getstate__ exclusion; pickling/deep-copy serialises the whole frame and pins it against GC, violating the project runtime-caches-break-pickle rule.
patch: Add to CompositeTargetDiscovery (discovery/__init__.py): __getstate__ returning self.__dict__.copy() with _df_ref and _auto_base_pool popped; __setstate__ that update()s then sets self._df_ref=None and self._auto_base_pool={}. Document on iter_transform that df must be passed explicitly after unpickling.

reason: A ~0.99-correlated sibling left in X_remaining inflates mi_y_compare while contributing little to mi_t, biasing mi_gain DOWN (conservative, no leak) for exactly the lag-family bases most likely genuine. Optional accuracy improvement gated on auto_base_dedup_corr_threshold; needs a per-base column mask (ties to D10 exclude-col) + biz_value measurement.

The else 'base outside the FE pool' arm (lines 376-379) is unreachable - every base in usable_features is in _col_index - AND methodologically wrong (the base would sit in its own x_remaining set). Dead, silently-wrong code.
patch: Replace the else body with: logger.error('[CompositeTargetDiscovery] invariant violated: base %r not in usable_features index; skipping (would leak base into its own x_remaining).', base); continue. Note the load-bearing invariant in _resolve_base_candidates.

reason: The degenerate-sample fallback returns list(usable_features)[:top_k], discarding hint_kept - the one signal (BD ablation) NOT derived from the broken screening sample, and hint features may not be within the first top_k of the arbitrary column order.
patch: Replace with: return (hint_kept + [c for c in usable_features if c not in hint_kept])[: self.config.auto_base_top_k].

reason: For 50<=n<5*mi_nbins every bin-MI value is silently 0.0 (mi_pair_bin/_mi_to_target_prebinned floor), so top-K degenerates to the alphabetical tiebreaker; undocumented at runtime. Log it; do not auto-shrink nbins (that changes numerics).
patch: In fit, after train_idx_screen is known: _eff_n=train_idx_screen.size; if self.config.mi_estimator=='bin' and _eff_n<5*int(self.config.mi_nbins): logger.warning('[CompositeTargetDiscovery] screening sample %d < 5*mi_nbins(%d): bin-MI inactive (all 0.0); ranking deferred to rerank/tiebreaker.', _eff_n, int(self.config.mi_nbins)).

reason: The gate admits on mi_gain_lcb but top-K ranking sorts on point s.mi_gain, so a high-variance big-point/low-LCB spec can outrank a stable better-LCB one - inconsistent with the gate's noise-floor rationale. Opt-in only (default bootstrap_n=0). The clean fix needs threading the entry-level lcb to the spec-sort (lcb is not on the spec), so document the intentional point-estimate ranking as a known minor inconsistency rather than ship a non-trivial plumbing change in a safe pass.

Already guarded: _rerank_inner_n_jobs=max(1,cpu//_rerank_n_jobs) threaded into _build_tiny_model(inner_n_jobs=). The 256-thread oversubscription path no longer exists; raw-baseline multiseed runs serially after the parallel loop (n_jobs default 1).
Min-seeds Wilcoxon guard present (_min_seeds_wilcoxon=ceil(log2(1/alpha)), skip+warn below it).
Seed-invariant splitter short-circuit to 1 repeat present in both multiseed wrappers.

patch: Add `and _td_report.get('picked_target_name') == _tname_disc` to the extreme-AR skip condition at :235-238 (only skip the target the AR stat was measured for). MLP twin: thread the same guard at extreme_ar_skip_decision's call site (pass is_picked_target=(target_name==picked_target_name) and require it in `fired`) -- same fix class, small follow-up.

reason: CONFIRMED: group-aware tiny-rerank is gated on the analyzer RECOMMENDATION (knob_overrides.split_config.prefer_group_aware) not the ACTUAL splitter config; a user who supplies group_ids + a group-aware splitter without the analyzer recommending it gets plain-KFold rerank (the documented prod failure). Fix needs a real 'splitter was group-aware' flag threaded from the suite boundary -- wiring/architectural, not a local guard.

Three silent-swallow gaps: (1) fit-exception (:541) and (2) row-mismatch (:319) log-only with NO composite_target_failures entry (every other skip path records one); (3) cache-replay `except Exception: _cached_specs=[]` (:451) has ZERO logging and leaves a metadata/behaviour divergence (specs claimed at :421, no T columns added). All cheap, no numerics.
patch: (1)+(2): before each `continue`, append a failures entry [{'name':_tname_disc,'kept':False,'rejected':True,'reason': <exc text / 'row-align mismatch y[N] vs df[M]'>}] to metadata['composite_target_failures'][str(_tt_disc)]. (3): change the bare except at :451 to log a WARNING with the exception + cache key, AND clear the just-written metadata['composite_target_specs'][str(_tt_disc)][_tname_disc]=[] so the claimed-specs/no-columns divergence is removed (full re-discovery fallback is the larger fix; the consistency clear + WARN removes the silent divergence now).

reason: pd.concat([sub, target_series], axis=1) copies blocks under pandas<3 / no-CoW -- a full peak-RAM duplication of the (100+GB-convention) train frame per regression target. Real, but the prescribed fixes (mutate-and-restore try/finally, or a y= kwarg into CompositeTargetDiscovery.fit) change a deliberately-anti-mutation path and need a before/after RAM measurement per perf-measure-first. Out of scope for a safe quick pass.

_LagPredictDeployableModel.predict returns the lag column verbatim incl. NaN at group starts; the OOF helper raises 'non-finite holdout predictions' (composite/ensemble/__init__.py:681) -> lag_predict dropped from the surviving set on EXACTLY the strong-AR grouped targets it exists for. Imputing non-finite with a fit-time train-median is a self-contained, clearly-correct edge guard (NaN preds are never useful; no copy/bench). Also fixes deploy-time NaN-lag rows.
patch: In __init__ add self._impute_value=None. In fit() extract the lag column the same way predict does and store self._impute_value = a finite np.nanmedian (else 0.0). In predict(), after building `col`, if `~np.isfinite(col)` has any True, copy col and set those entries to self._impute_value (fallback to nanmedian(col) or 0.0 when _impute_value is None, e.g. the un-fit injected instance). _impute_value is a fitted attr (not in get_params) so sklearn.clone re-fits it per OOF fold (train-only).

reason: CONFIRMED: both watchdog blocks build a 1-D base from the single base_column; for linear_residual_multi (in _ADDITIVE_TRANSFORMS, :548) inverse/forward need the (n,1+K) matrix -> ValueError swallowed at DEBUG (:587,:650) -> ZERO watchdog coverage for the spec family most prone to wrapper-math bugs. Fix = build the multi-column base in both blocks + a new regression test that corrupts a multi-base wrapper and asserts the WARNING fires. Test-gap + 2-site matrix build, beyond a one-line guard.

is_composite_target_name(model_name) runs on the concatenated f'{target_name} {model_name} {cur_target_name}' (passed at _phase_train_one_target_model_setup.py:539), so a raw target containing '-diff-'/'-ratio-' is mis-stamped MTRESID. Reporting-only; select_target has exactly ONE caller and cur_target_name is available there.
patch: Add param `cur_target_name: Optional[str] = None` to select_target; at :120 use `_is_composite = is_composite_target_name(cur_target_name or model_name)`. At model_setup.py:538 pass `cur_target_name=cur_target_name`.

reason: _mttr/_mtts are computed on the y TEST slice (y_target) but the chart header renders 'MTTR/MTTS=' (suite convention: MTTR = TRAIN-split mean) -> cross-reading against MTTR= log lines gives inconsistent numbers under drift. Also _human_inner (:64) is computed and never used.
patch: At :70 relabel the header from 'MTTR/MTTS=' to 'test_mean/test_std=' (or 'MTeR/MTeS='); delete the unused `_human_inner = _inner_class.replace(...)` line at :64.

reason: recover_composite_y_scale_metrics is annotated `-> dict[int, np.ndarray]` but delegates to _run_composite_target_wrapping whose returned cache is keyed by (id(wrapper), id(frame), shape) tuples (wrapping.py:245,457).
patch: Change the return annotation at :68 from `dict[int, np.ndarray]` to `dict[tuple, np.ndarray]`.

reason: 'wrapped %d model(s)' logs len(_entries), counting entries that had no predict, were already wrapped (continue at :294), or failed from_fitted_inner (continue at :318) -> operator can be told 'wrapped 4' when 1 stayed T-scale.
patch: Init `_n_wrapped = 0` before the entry loop; increment only where the wrapper is actually assigned (after the :328/:331/:333 success branches); log `_n_wrapped` instead of `len(_entries)` at :336.

reason: CONFIRMED parity gaps vs single-target: (a) MTR ensemble is APPENDED into models[tt][orig_tname] (:281) not a dedicated _CT_ENSEMBLE__{name} key -> it appears alongside its own component siblings (votenrank/per-entry tables misread it); (b) SimpleNamespace lacks metrics={} (single-target sets it at __init__.py:880); (c) no ensembles_chosen['cross_target'] stamp. (a) is a registration-shape change needing downstream save/predict verification; (b)/(c) are cheap but best landed with (a).

Class docstring (:31 'Weights are normalised to sum to 1') and the weights property doc (:108-112 'Each column sums to 1.0 ... post-normalisation (nnls)') contradict the code, which DELIBERATELY keeps RAW NNLS weights (:162-174 rationale). A maintainer trusting the doc could re-add normalisation and regress fits.
patch: Update the class docstring (:31) and the weights property docstring (:108-112) to state nnls keeps RAW (non-normalised) NNLS weights -- convex/sum-to-1 only for equal_mean -- referencing the rationale at :164-173.

reason: When filtered_train_idx is None, _y_arr[filtered_train_idx] becomes _y_arr[None] -> shape (1,n), so the guard fires the confusing 'y[1] vs filtered_train_df[N]' instead of stating the index is missing. The BaselineDiagnostics block 5 lines up (:279-281) already handles None explicitly.
patch: Before `_y_train_aligned = _y_arr[filtered_train_idx]` at :318 add `if filtered_train_idx is None:` -> log a clear 'filtered_train_idx missing; skipping discovery for %s' warning, append a failures entry (per I12), and continue.

reason: _arr[~_fin] = median(finite) imputes a component whose train predict FAILED with the MEDIAN RMSE -> from_train_metrics grants it mid-pack weight on the fallback path. Safer = impute max(finite) (worst) or drop. Only reachable on the double-failure path (OOF failed AND component scoring failed); changing imputation alters fallback weights so wants a small eyeball/bench before flipping. Low reach, low value-per-risk for the quick pass.

reason: The dummy-floor / prescreen / oof_weighted gates read the strongest-dummy primary_metric generically and compare against component RMSEs; today the regression primary is hard-coded val_RMSE (baselines/_dummy_metrics_pick_plot.py:108) so units match.
